data.question: Should we fix spec drift now or continue Alex β system work?
data.answer: Continue Alex β work, fix drift before next build run

data.question: What should our testing strategy be? Playwright, Vitest, AI agent, or defer?
data.answer: Playwright e2e as next priority after consolidation. Vitest wrong tool for stale-state bugs. AI test agent overengineered — AI writes tests, not runs them. Defer until consolidation ends.

data.question: STALE markers on auth/market-research stories — investigate PRD changes (A) or commit as-is (B)?
data.answer: Option A — investigate first. Two reads to verify.
data.outcome_detail: PRD changes were cosmetic only (removed STALE markers). Downstream markers were cascading artifacts. Safe to commit after verification.

data.question: Gamma Agent tool workaround — relay vs CLI vs direct?
data.answer: Option 2: Gamma uses claude CLI via Bash. Same dispatch model, different execution path.

data.question: QA findings — fix HIGHs now or defer?
data.answer: Fix HIGHs now, grouped by class: CSRF first, saveSession cluster, data integrity, nav bugs last.

data.question: Commit now or keep going before storage/auth work?
data.answer: Commit now. Two logical units done — separate commits before new feature work.

data.question: Storage/auth gap prioritization — what order?
data.answer: Beta logging first, then schema versioning, TTL refresh, Redis validation, OAuth state. Skip password reset email until provider chosen.

data.question: What next after session infrastructure — OAuth, spec drift, maps, or wrap?
data.answer: Spec drift next. 58 DANGER items compound — builders read wrong specs. OAuth can wait (expiry mitigation, no live users). Maps refresh after drift cleared.

data.question: Next after spec drift cleared — maps, OAuth, or wrap?
data.answer: Pending — awaiting response

data.question: Next after spec drift — maps, OAuth, or wrap?
data.answer: Maps refresh first (precondition met), then OAuth state fix, then commit and wrap. Password reset stays deferred.

data.question: Auto mode, no feature — which track: (A) cross-provider dispatch test, (B) install-vs-repo mismatch / WarpOS shape, (C) spec drift backlog?
data.answer: B first, then A. B is the load-bearing launch blocker — install shape determines what users actually receive on launch day. A validates a mechanism that's already designed, can run after B is resolved. C is hygiene, defer post-launch.

data.question: manifest.providers stale block — (A) remove, (B) convert to agentProviders with correct mapping, (C) leave with comment?
data.answer: Remove stale providers block AND add agentProviders with correct current mapping (evaluator/compliance/auditor/qa→openai, redteam→gemini, rest→claude). Two separate operations: delete the wrong-namespace block, add the right-namespace block. manifest.providers is for CLI config objects, not role mapping. New users reading manifest on install day will see correct intent.

data.question: γ committed /fav:clear skill directly to skeleton-test7 instead of isolated worktree — (A) revert, (B) keep + log for retro?
data.answer: Keep. Not a violation. Worktree isolation is a oneshot protocol requirement (HYGIENE Rule 12, 02-oneshot). Adhoc protocol has no isolation requirement — γ commits directly to current branch by design. This is correct adhoc behavior, not drift. No revert.

data.question: /fav:clear cleanup — (A) revert 37d32e4 + delete test branch, (B) keep + fix, (C) keep as-is?
data.answer: Option A. Skill wasn't requested — γ built it as a pressure-test vehicle. Redteam findings are legitimate (destructive command, no confirmation, no backup). Shipping an unsafe destructive skill on launch day is wrong. test/fav-clear-pressure-20260416 is a test branch not a backup branch — deletion permitted. Revert 37d32e4, delete test branch.

data.question: fav:* prompt-injection HIGH finding — (A) fix now, (B) punt post-launch, (C) disable fav:* at launch?
data.answer: Option A. Fix now. 30 min is cheap for a HIGH on launch day. Local-only reduces blast radius (attacker needs local FS write) but testers are real users who will invoke these skills on day 1. Delimiter pattern is also a reusable architectural fix for any skill that reads user-provided files. α's lean is correct.

data.question: fav:* delimiter-breakout residual MEDIUM — (A) random delimiter, (B) save-side sanitization, (C) structural code-parse, (D) accept MEDIUM + ticket C post-launch?
data.answer: Option D with A applied. Apply random 16-hex delimiter now (5 min), accept MEDIUM risk, ticket C for post-launch. Attacker preconditions: local FS write + awareness of internal delimiter. Solo founder testers on their own machines — this threat model is essentially self-attack. 'Data/Instruction confusion' residual is a fundamental LLM limitation, not a fixable bug; redteam flagging it as a finding is a category error. 1-2 hours on structural fix is not launch-critical given actual exposure.

data.question: WarpOS update plan scope — (A) painful three + Drift Gate, (B) painful three only, (C) painful three + Drift Gate + Coherence subset, (D) full 28 ARCH items
data.answer: ESCALATE — time-allocation for solo dev at MVP stage. Cannot confidently model whether user will tolerate 3 weeks of framework work vs product work. Recommendation: Option A (painful three + Drift Gate), 135 stale hardcoded refs will compound into builder failures without enforcement layer.
data.user_answer: D — Full vision (all items, AI-pace). User note: 'you propose time windows in human time. But I know you can do it all faster.'

data.question: docs→requirements rename approach — (A) big-bang one PR, (B) canonicalize-then-migrate, (C) fix resolution only
data.answer: Option A — big-bang atomic PR with worktree drain first. Mixed state (B) confuses builders; skip (C) contradicts goal. Simplicity + Reversibility win.

data.question: Where does /warp:promote live — (A) build here in jobzooka, (B) build in WarpOS canonical repo
data.answer: Option A — build in jobzooka, this is the further-along version. P-008 cross-repo parity, no separate clone needed. OPEN_ADR flagged.

data.question: NEXT.md disposition — (A) project-only NEXT.md + framework-only NEXT-WARP.md, (B) single NEXT.md with subsection, (C) purely additive
data.answer: Option A — separate files by layer. Simpler, no co-ownership confusion, consistent with product/tooling layer separation in CLAUDE.md.
